label: AppleScript • live many-literal slot selection
query: In Notes, create a note named 'Digest' and set its body to the provided intro followed by the provided summary.
notes: Capability/scale lane: fifteen named literals are provided (more than the prompt's per-item preview cap of 12, so 'summary' and some snippets appear name-only). Among thirteen distractor 'snippet' blocks, the task names exactly two — 'intro' and 'summary' (summary is past the preview cap). Scores that the real 16B references those two via their {{name}} placeholders (mustUsePlaceholders) and composes them in order without grabbing the distractors or inventing text (Grok rubric). Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

contents.summary: Bottom line: on track for the June release.

rubric: The note body is composed of the provided intro block followed by the provided summary block.
It uses those two provided blocks — not the distractor 'snippet' blocks — and does not invent body text.
